content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON
CONNELLY
HAGEL
FONG &
LEIDIGH

FPPC
Nov 24 12:28 PM '92

November 20, 1992


Wayne Imberi
Technical Assistance Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE

Dear Mr. Imberi:

This letter seeks written confirmation of
telephone advice which you provided to me last week. I
asked the appropriate method for registering and
reporting for lobbying activities in the following
factual circumstance.

FACTS

XYZ is an Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(6)
tax-exempt trade association. Members of XYZ pay dues
to XYZ for various activities conducted by the trade
association. In addition, XYZ raised revenues through
various commercial activities, including the conduct of
its annual convention. For tax purposes, this income
was called "unrelated business income." For reasons of
its tax-exempt status, XYZ was advised to create a
separate "for profit" corporate entity to conduct its
commercial income-generating activities because XYZ
planned to expand those activities.

Consequently, XYZ has now "spun-off" a wholly
owned subsidiary, known as XYZ Services, Inc. to
undertake those activities. In addition, XYZ Services,
Inc. has contracted with XYZ to provide the staff
services for the trade association which XYZ was
providing directly before the "spin-off." These
services include, but are not limited to, employing two
in-house lobbyists, and contracting with an outside
lobbying firm.

QUESTIONS AND ADVICE

Based upon the foregoing facts, I asked you
whether XYZ and XYZ Services, Inc. should register as a
lobbyist employer, listing both names as "affiliated

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
GEORGE WATERS
DIANE M. FISHBURN

Of Counsel
LLOYD G. CONNELLY

CapitolBank Center
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Telephone (916) 442-2952
Facsimile (916) 442-1280

Wayne Imberi
November 20, 1992
Page 2

entities" or whether XYZ Services, Inc. should register as a
lobbying firm and have XYZ listed as the lobbyist employer.

You consulted with others on the staff and advised that the
staff believes the correct method is to have both XYZ and XYZ
Services, Inc. register as one lobbyist employer, with the two
names being reflected on the registration and showing that XYZ
Services, Inc. is a wholly owned subsidiary. Under that
approach, the two in-house lobbyists will continue to register as
such, but with the two entities shown as the lobbyist employer.
The one outside lobbying firm will list the two entities as its
lobbyist employer, as well.

Please provide written confirmation of this advice. If you
have any questions, please give me a call. If this letter is not
fully in accord with your advice, please advise me at once.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

REL:jlh

These services include, but are not limited to, employing two in-house lobbyists, and contracting with an outside lobbying firm.
